---
title: A Sample Article Using Quarto with Standard LaTeX Article Class
format:
  xarticle-pdf:
    keep-tex: true
author:
  - id: xz
    name: Xin Zhang
    affiliations:
      - name: Southeast University
        department: School of Mathematics
        city: Nanjing
        country: China
        postal-code: 211189
    orcid: 0000-0001-6670-5351
    email: xzhangseu@seu.edu.cn
    url: https://math.seu.edu.cn/zx_en/list.htm
    attributes:
      corresponding: true
    bio: |
      Author biography text goes here.
  - name: John Doe
    affiliations: 
      - name: Anonymous University
    bio: |
      Author biography text goes here.
abstract: |
  This document demonstrates how to use Quarto with the standard LaTeX article
  document class. It produces PDF output suitable for preprints,
  working papers, and general academic writing.
keywords: [Quarto, LaTeX, article, Pandoc, template]
bibliography: bibliography.bib
date: 2023-06-23
---

# Introduction {#sec-intro}

中国人民This file is a sample article demonstrating Quarto's capabilities with the standard
LaTeX `article` document class. It supports PDF (via LaTeX) output.
It is assumed that the reader has a basic working knowledge of stochastic calculus @karatzas1991brownian
and stochastic control theory @yong1999sch.






$$
\begin{aligned}
&\forall x \in \mathbb{R},\quad 
\exists\, y \in \mathbb{N}:\quad 
\alpha + \beta = \gamma \neq \delta \leq \epsilon \approx \zeta \\
&\sum_{i=1}^{n} x_i^2 + \prod_{j=1}^{m} y_j^2 = \int_{a}^{b} f(x)\,dx \\
&\lim_{n\to\infty} \frac{1}{n} = 0, \qquad
\nabla \cdot \vec{E} = \frac{\rho}{\varepsilon_0} \\
&A = \begin{pmatrix} a_{11} & a_{12} \\ a_{21} & a_{22} \end{pmatrix}, \qquad
B = \begin{bmatrix} b_1 \\ b_2 \end{bmatrix} \\
&S = \{ x \mid x > 0 \}, \qquad
f: X \to Y, \qquad
x \mapsto f(x) \\
&\bigcup_{i=1}^n A_i \cap \bigcap_{j=1}^m B_j, \qquad
A \subseteq B, \quad A \supset B \\
&\mathcal{L} = \mathcal{F}(\mathbb{R}), \qquad
\mathfrak{g} \cong \mathfrak{h} \\
&\boxed{E = mc^2}
\end{aligned}
$$


::: {#def-continuous}

## 连续性

设函数 $f: D \subseteq \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}$，$x_0 \in D$。如果对于任意 $\epsilon > 0$，存在 $\delta > 0$，使得当 $|x - x_0| < \delta$ 且 $x \in D$ 时，有 $|f(x) - f(x_0)| < \epsilon$，则称 $f$ 在 $x_0$ 处连续。

:::

### 定理环境

::: {#thm-mvt}

## 中值定理

设函数 $f: [a,b] \to \mathbb{R}$ 连续，在 $(a,b)$ 内可导。则存在 $\xi \in (a,b)$，使得

$$
f'(\xi) = \frac{f(b) - f(a)}{b - a}
$${#eq-einstein}

:::

参考定理 @thm-mvt 可知...

### 引理环境

::: {#lem-fermat}

## 费马引理

设函数 $f$ 在 $x_0$ 处可导，由公式 @eq-einstein 知 $x_0$ 是 $f$ 的极值点，则 $f'(x_0) = 0$。

:::

### 推论环境

::: {#cor-zero}

## 导数零点定理

若函数 $f$ 在区间 $I$ 上可导且 $f'(x) = 0$ 对所有 $x \in I$ 成立，则 $f$ 在 $I$ 上为常数函数。

:::

::: {#thm-residue .theorem}
## Residue Theorem
Let $f$ be analytic in a simply connected domain $D$. Then for any closed contour $\gamma \subset D$,
$$
\oint_\gamma f(z)\,dz = 2\pi i \sum_{k} \mathrm{Res}(f, z_k)
$$
:::

This template supports standard Quarto cross-references. You can refer to Theorem [@thm-residue] in the text.

# Markdown Basics {#sec-markdown}

The template is built on Quarto and Pandoc, leveraging standard LaTeX tools for PDF output.

## Equations

Inline math: $E = mc^2$. Display math:

$$
\frac{\partial u}{\partial t} = \alpha \nabla^2 u
$$

## Theorems and Proofs

::: {#lem-key .lemma}
## Key Lemma
If $f$ is continuous on $[a,b]$, then $f$ is integrable on $[a,b]$.
:::

::: {#cor-easy .corollary}
## Easy Corollary
Every continuous function on a closed interval is bounded.
:::

::: {#def-model .definition}
## Model Definition
A stochastic process $\{X_t\}_{t \ge 0}$ is a collection of random variables indexed by time.
:::

## Lists

1. First item
2. Second item
3. Third item

- Bullet one
- Bullet two

## Tables

| Variable | Description | Unit |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| $x$       | Position    | m    |
| $v$       | Velocity    | m/s  |
| $a$       | Acceleration| m/s² |

: A sample table {#tbl-sample}

# Bibliography

This template uses `natbib` with the `plainnat-doi` bibliography style for author-year citations @karatzas1991brownian.

# Acknowledgment {-}

This should be a simple paragraph before the References to
thank those individuals and institutions who have supported
your work on this article.

[]{.appendix options="An Appendix"}

Use the `[]{.appendix options="An Appendix"}` markup if you have a single appendix.
Do not use `\section{}` after `\appendix` in LaTeX.
